title: Commersonia densiflora
intro: Commersonia densiflora, commonly known as the 'Dense-flowered Commersonia', is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and skin disorders. Bioactive constituents of Commersonia densiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditional herbal preparations made from the plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to promote general well-being and alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
taxonomy: Commersonia densiflora is a member of the family Malvaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Commersonia, which is a part of the order Malvales. The species Commersonia densiflora is native to Australia and is also known as the cluster bush or cluster hibiscus. The classification of Commersonia densiflora includes the kingdom Plantae, which encompasses all plants.
